title: Taiwan braces for Super Typhoon Bavi
description: Residents in northern Taiwan are preparing for Typhoon Bavi as authorities evacuate thousands of people, shut schools and businesses, and brace for torrential rain, flooding and winds of up to 155 km/h.The storm is expected to make landfall in southeast China on Saturday after already causing deadly landslides in the Philippines and widespread damage in Guam and the Northern Mariana Islands.

title: Spain wildfires: Deadliest blaze in 20 years
description: At least 12 people have died and 23 remain missing after Spain's deadliest wildfire in two decades tore through the southern region of Almeria, with authorities saying the blaze may have started when a power line fell onto dry vegetation. As Europe faces an early and intense fire season, Spain now accounts for around 40% of all land burned across the continent, with firefighters still battling the flames.

title: The Bayeux Tapestry's risky journey across the Channel
description: Nearly 1,000 years after chronicling the Norman conquest of England, the Bayeux Tapestry is making history once again. The fragile 11th-century embroidery has arrived in London on an unprecedented loan to the British Museum, prompting delight and concern in equal measure. Celebrated as one of Europe's greatest medieval treasures, its journey has reignited debates over heritage, diplomacy and the risks of moving delicate cultural artefacts.

title: How top Syrian diplomat Asaad al-Shaibani’s road to Damascus is shaking the Mideast
description: Syrian Foreign Minister Asaad al-Shaibani’s diplomatic motorcade hit the road shortly after his appointment. From Riyadh to Beirut, Washington to Moscow, Shaibani has been at the forefront of President Ahmed al-Sharaa’s bid to open a new chapter in Mideast history. But the rebel official-turned-diplomat may find Damascus does not hold all the cards in a volatile region.

title: Medieval masterpiece Bayeux Tapestry arrives in London
description: The Bayeux Tapestry has arrived in London for the first time in more than 900 years after a highly secure and complex journey from France to the British Museum, as FRANCE 24's Rochelle Ferguson-Bouyahi explains. The 11th-century masterpiece depicting the Battle of Hastings will be displayed from September 2026 to July 2027, in a historic loan hailed as a symbol of Franco-British ties.

title: Can Ukraine really build its own Patriots?
description: Donald Trump’s promise to let Ukraine manufacture Patriot air defence systems could strengthen Kyiv’s capabilities in the long term, but experts warn it will not solve the country’s immediate battlefield needs. Building production lines, training workers and securing supplies could take years, while Ukraine continues to face daily Russian attacks.

title: Foreign workers feel the heat as Japan hardens nationalist stance
description: On this week's edition of Access Asia, we bring you a report from Japan, where visa fees have been increased for foreign nationals applying for permanent residency. The government is also considering introducing a language requirement. While Japan needs foreign workers to help offset an ageing population and a declining birth rate, the nationalist government has continued to tighten its immigration policies.

title: France into World Cup semi-finals : 'When it gets a bit difficult, they can hang in there'
description: France are through to the World Cup semi-finals, and freelance journalist Francis Collings, based in Paris, analyses Les Bleus’ chances of going all the way. He says France have shown they can withstand pressure when matches become difficult, a key strength in their bid to lift the trophy.

title: Cardi B, Teyana Taylor and Bad Bunny brave Paris heat

title: Sudan: El-Obeid civilians trapped under RSF siege

FRANCE 24 correspondent Bastien Renoul spoke to residents trapped inside, who describe bombed water infrastructure, constant strikes and growing fears of famine as food supplies run out.

title: Gulf, Arab states welcome US-Iran deal to end war, reopen Hormuz
description:
DUBAI: Gulf and Arab states welcomed on Monday a US-Iran agreement to end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, expressing hope that the deal will pave the way for lasting regional stability and broader diplomatic engagement.

	Saudi Arabia welcomed the agreement between Washington and Tehran to halt military operations and begin detailed negotiations over a 60-day period aimed at reaching a permanent settlement.

title: Oil hits 3-month low as US, Iran reach peace deal to reopen Strait of Hormuz

SINGAPORE: Oil prices ​slipped to a three-month low on Monday after US President Donald Trump and Iran's deputy foreign minister said ‌they had reached an initial deal to end the war and to resume traffic through the Strait of Hormuz.

description: Details were scant about a project that may amount to another European weapons-coordination umbrella rather than new technology development.

title: Polish defense giant PGZ to produce Anduril’s Barracuda cruise missile
description: Local production could make the weapon eligible for purchases by other European nations through a European Union lending scheme, according to PGZ.

title: Mali rebels attack convoy of Russian reinforcements headed to besieged military camp
description: Tuareg separatists from the Azawad Liberation Front and their jihadist allies attacked a convoy of Russian soldiers and Malian troops Thursday as they were en route to reinforce a military camp in the northern town of Anefis.

title: Iran claims US strike hit near nuclear powerplant
description: The United States and Iran are continuing to trade strikes on July 9 for a second straight day. after President Donald Trump said the ceasefire between the two countries was "dead". According to Iran's health ministry, the two days of US attacks have killed at least 14 people and injured more than 70. In response, Tehran said it had resumed its targeting of US assets in Kuwait, Bahrain and Qatar.

title: Factory fire kills at least 28 in China's 'shoe capital'
description: Footage shows huge flames rising from a building and thick, black smoke rising into the sky.

title: Former US Olympian pleads not guilty in DC reflecting pool vandalism case
description: Former Olympic canoeist David Hearn denies damaging Donald Trump’s Washington, DC reflecting pool renovation.

title: A quest for closure: In search of the missing after Venezuela’s earthquakes
description: Tens of thousands of people remain unaccounted for nearly two weeks after a pair of deadly earthquakes struck the coast.
